name: PR-stack Review Response (round 2: PRs #22-#24) -- NDEBT-016 pipe fix + validate.sh doc-only truth-sync + debt registration

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Feature 047 is a review-response overhead feature (not a handover deliverable): it answers CodeRabbit's round-2 comments across PRs #22/#23/#24 (30 comments total; PR #21 was skipped by CodeRabbit -- 291 files over the 150 limit -- no feature action) as a single new commit at the stack tip. Per the planner's AH-2 triage (.agent/planning_notes_047.md), the 30 findings resolve into: 2 FIX (both confirmed still present at HEAD 6db8363), 6 DEBT findings collapsed into 2 registered rows (NDEBT-021 Low, NDEBT-022 Medium), and 20 EXPLAIN + 2 DUPLICATE (22 ready-to-post PR replies, no repository change). This contract covers EXACTLY the planner's scope: two files, docs/planning/DEBT.md and tools/validate.sh -- nothing else. FIX 1 escapes three raw pipes inside NDEBT-016's regex code-span (which currently makes GFM mis-parse the row as 8 cells instead of 5); FIX 2 is two DOC-ONLY validate.sh rewordings (a stale --target-exclusivity claim contradicted by C12's default-sweep fixture reads, and a comment implying --target works for the ecosystem families when NDEBT-015 records it doesn't) -- explicitly NOT touching line 319's already-correct phrasing (the refuted part of that finding).
design_notes.line319_literal_discrepancy: feature_list_005.json's own acceptance test 10 asserts `grep -Fq 'independently inspectable via a direct python3+jsonschema invocation' tools/validate.sh`. Live-verified against the REAL, current, unmodified tools/validate.sh (which this contract must not touch at line 319): that exact single-line, backtick-free phrase can NEVER match, because the real text wraps across two source lines ('...already independently inspectable via a\n      direct `python3`+`jsonschema` invocation...') and wraps `python3`/`jsonschema` in backticks -- confirmed live: `grep -Fq` against the real file returns not-found. This is a textual discrepancy in the feature list's OWN acceptance-test wording (not something 047 is scoped to fix, and not a defect in the target file itself, which is correctly left untouched), and including the exact broken literal verbatim would make this contract's own verification entry permanently unsatisfiable for any implementation, correct or not -- precisely the round-0 HIGH-severity defect class contract 045 was rejected for, and the same class of discrepancy contract 046 disclosed for its payload-mode literal. Verification entry 9 therefore asserts the CORRECT, real, unchanging, whitespace-collapsed phrase (matching the planner's own stated house rule: 'content checks that could markdown-wrap must be whitespace-collapsed before matching') instead of reproducing a knowingly-false single-line literal. Flagged here explicitly for validator/evaluator visibility and disposition at Mode A review. Adversarially re-proven: a scratch fixture that actually tampers with line 319's wording is correctly caught by the corrected check (FAILS), confirming it remains genuinely load-bearing, not weakened into a no-op.
design_notes.head_caret_timing_note: Several of feature_list_005.json's own mandated literals (the NDEBT-016 and help-text anti-vacuity companions, entries 4 and 6; the tip-diff scope-confinement literal folded into entry 17's note) are written as `git show HEAD^:...` / `git diff ... HEAD^ HEAD ...`, anticipating execution AFTER the orchestrator commits this feature's changes as the new stack-tip commit (at which point HEAD = the 047 commit and HEAD^ = 6db8363, the pre-047 tip) -- exactly the same timing pattern contract 046 established and disclosed for its own mandated HEAD-caret literals. At CONTRACT-PROPOSAL and IMPLEMENTATION time (this dispatch), no such commit exists yet: entries 4 and 6 happen to already pass today (the raw-pipe regex and the stale help text both genuinely existed at 6db8363's own parent commit dd4e234 too, confirmed live), which is coincidental correctness, not the checks' real load-bearing window; the tip-diff literal cannot be meaningfully evaluated pre-commit at all (there is no 047 commit yet for HEAD^..HEAD to span). REVISED at revision 1 (per design_notes.exact_transform_fix): entry 10 (validate.sh doc-only proof) is NO LONGER HEAD-caret-anchored -- it now compares the transformed CURRENT HEAD (6db8363, the real pre-047 tip, always resolvable pre-commit) against the working tree, so it is meaningful and load-bearing RIGHT NOW, not timing-deferred; it correctly FAILS pre-implementation (working tree == unmodified HEAD) and correctly PASSES once exactly the two pinned substitutions land, independently re-proven in a disposable scratch git repository (compliant PASSES; all three of the evaluator's tampers FAIL; a mixed compliant+tamper commit FAILS; whitespace-only drift FAILS, confirming byte-identity is intentionally strict for a payload-shipped file -- see design_notes.exact_transform_fix for full detail). This contract's real, load-bearing, pre-commit proof of doc-only-ness is therefore now entry 10 itself (no longer merely a diff-pattern search) plus entry 17's `vlib_scope_guard` invocation (proven clean against the real, current tree). The remaining mandated HEAD-caret literals (entries 4, 6, and the tip-diff note in entry 17) are retained verbatim so they are honored once the orchestrator's later commit makes them meaningful, per the identical treatment contract 046 gave its own timing-deferred mandated literals.
design_notes.planner_feature_list_edit_tolerance: The current working tree already carries an UNCOMMITTED, pre-existing modification to `.agent/feature_list_005.json` (the planner's own addition of the feature-047 entry, made before this contract was authored) plus a new untracked `.agent/planning_notes_047.md` (this contract's own source material). Neither is part of this contract's scope of change (per the explicit non-goal mirroring feature 046's own) and neither should be flagged as a scope violation. Per the contract-046 Mode-A round-0 BLOCKER lesson (whitelist a contract's own tracked deliverables, not just the planner's pre-existing edit) -- APPLIED PROACTIVELY here even though it does not literally recur: THIS feature's own deliverables (docs/planning/DEBT.md, tools/validate.sh) are NOT under `.agent/` at all, so entry 16's tracked-diff tolerance correctly contains ONLY the planner's pre-existing feature_list_005.json edit, with no contract-owned .agent path needing to be added -- dry-run-proven against the real repository (which currently carries exactly this pre-existing state) and, for the adversarial direction (an unauthorized edit to a DIFFERENT committed contract, e.g. .agent/contracts/046.json), against a disposable scratch git repository.
design_notes.e2e_and_sweep_dry_run_scope: Both compliance-sweep entries (18, 19) and the e2e bootstrap entry (20) are 'must remain green' invariants, not 'must become true' gate-dependent checks: this feature's edits to tools/validate.sh are doc-only (comments/help-text bytes only, per entry 10's own structural proof), so the sweeps and the e2e test are expected to behave identically before and after. This was proven THREE ways without touching the real repository: (1) live-verified the real, current, unmodified tree already passes both sweeps (12/12, 10/10) and the e2e test, establishing the baseline; (2) built a disposable, non-git scratch copy of the full repository tree (every directory the validator/e2e tooling reads: standard/, templates/, schema/, tools/, registry/, methodology/, ecosystem/, docs/, .agent/, .github/, plus the root files) with the doc-only-edited tools/validate.sh substituted in, and RAN the real validator against it -- both sweeps report byte-identical SUMMARY lines (12 passed/0 failed; SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed/0 failed) before and after the substitution, the strongest available proof of behavioral neutrality; (3) the e2e bootstrap test itself requires real git history (it clones itself via a local `file://` URL) that a non-git scratch copy structurally cannot provide (confirmed live: it fails with 'not a git repository' in the scratch copy, an environmental artifact of the dry-run harness, not a defect) -- so entry 20's full post-implementation re-confirmation is necessarily Mode B/QA's responsibility against the real, committed tree, exactly as entries 18/19 also require a final re-confirmation post-implementation despite already being proven behaviorally inert here.
design_notes.estimated_lines_derivation: docs/planning/DEBT.md: 1 line changed (NDEBT-016's three pipes escaped in place) + 1 line changed (version bump) + 2 new lines (NDEBT-021, NDEBT-022 rows, each a single physical line matching house convention) = ~4 lines. tools/validate.sh: Spot A replaces 2 old lines with 3 new lines (~5 diff lines) + Spot B replaces 1 old line with 3 new lines (~4 diff lines) = ~9 diff lines. Total ~13 lines -- roughly half of feature_list_005.json's own 25-line a-priori estimate, reflecting a narrowly-scoped pipe-escape plus two short doc rewordings plus two DEBT rows, not new capability work. Per-feature scope-budget check: rolling average of the last 3 completed features (044=~304, 045=~77, 046=~20, all per their own committed ledgers) = ~134, 3x = ~402; ~13 is far under -- no per-feature flag. Cumulative check: well within the phase's existing 3500-base/4550-ceiling scope budget given the tiny size of this feature.
design_notes.dry_run_proof_summary: All of the following were proven WITHOUT creating, editing, or deleting anything in the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework, other than this contract file itself: (1) `git show HEAD:...` / `git show HEAD^:...` (read-only) confirmed the anti-vacuity preconditions for both files. (2) Disposable scratch copies of DEBT.md and tools/validate.sh were mutated to simulate the exact planned implementation (the three-pipe escape; the two new DEBT rows; the two validate.sh rewordings), and every mandated-literal and this-contract's-own verification command was re-run against BOTH the untouched pre-implementation copies (correctly FAILS on every gate-dependent entry) and the compliant post-implementation copies (correctly PASSES all of them). (3) The NDEBT-016 cell-count parse (entry 2) and the no-raw-pipes guard (entry 15) were each independently adversarially probed: a fixture with the fix genuinely applied passes; a fixture with a stray un-escaped pipe deliberately reintroduced into a new row correctly FAILS. (4) The doc-only diff guard (entry 10) was proven in a disposable scratch git repository across three commits: base -> doc-only fix (passes) -> a further commit that also touches a `check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures` invocation line (correctly FAILS, since the changed line still contains the `check_c[0-9]` token regardless of context). (5) The line-319 preservation check (entry 9, corrected per design_notes.line319_literal_discrepancy) was proven in both directions: passes against both the pre- and post-implementation fixtures (line 319 is never touched by either); a deliberately tampered fixture (line 319's wording changed) correctly FAILS. (6) The .agent immutability guard (entry 16) and scope guard (entry 17) were each proven against the real, current tree (clean) and adversarially against disposable scratch git repositories: an unauthorized tracked .agent edit (to a different committed contract, .agent/contracts/046.json) correctly FAILS the immutability guard; an out-of-scope shipped-file edit (a CHANGELOG.md-analog) correctly FAILS the scope guard; both revert to PASS. (7) The row-count-delta bound (entry 13) was proven in a disposable scratch git repository across three states: pre-implementation (delta=0) FAILS; the compliant two-new-row state (delta=2) PASSES; an unauthorized third-row state (delta=3) FAILS. (8) The two compliance-sweep entries and the e2e entry were live-re-run against the real, current, unmodified tree (currently green) AND, for the sweeps specifically, against a disposable non-git full-tree scratch copy with the doc-only-edited validate.sh substituted in -- both sweeps report byte-identical SUMMARY lines before and after, the strongest available pre-implementation proof of behavioral neutrality (see design_notes.e2e_and_sweep_dry_run_scope for the e2e test's own git-history dependency, which necessarily defers its full re-confirmation to Mode B/QA). (9) The cross-feature invariants (045 checklist SHA, 045 P0/P1 scan, 046's NDEBT-019/020 preservation, release-notes hash, run_state.json, manifest.json, tag count) were all re-run against both the real, current tree and the compliant post-implementation DEBT.md fixture, confirming NDEBT-021/022's Low/Medium severities do not regress the zero-P0/P1 invariant and that NDEBT-019/020 remain exactly as 046 registered them. Nothing under the real repository tree was created, edited, or deleted to produce any of these proofs, other than this contract file.
design_notes.exact_transform_fix: REVISED at revision 1 per @evaluator round-0 HIGH finding: revision 0's entry 10 was a diff-line pattern search for three token classes (a shell function-definition brace, a check_c[0-9] invocation token, and the exit expression failed.*-eq 0) -- structurally blind to any change made INSIDE a check function's own embedded heredoc body, since such a change touches none of the three watched token classes. The evaluator constructed and committed THREE distinct real behavioral tampers in a disposable scratch clone, each alongside no other change: (1) narrowing check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures's heredoc NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex from `(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)` to `(?:^|_)(?:neg)(?:_|\.)` (silently dropping 'invalid'-token fixture-polarity detection); (2) narrowing the heredoc's fixture glob from `tools/fixtures/{family}_*.json` to `tools/fixtures/{family}_positive_*.json` (silently skipping all negative fixtures for every family); (3) DEFANGING THE DORMANCY GUARD itself (NDEBT-009's own regression-prevention meta-check, appending `and False` to both `if not positive_paths` / `if not negative_paths` conditions). All three FALSELY passed revision-0's entry 10; tamper (3) is additionally invisible to the ENTIRE 27-entry suite including the sweep-green invariants (entries 18/19), since none of the 15 currently-shipped fixtures happen to trigger the dormancy guard's firing condition either way today -- a real, safety-relevant regression to a v0.7.0-consumer-payload-shipped file could have landed completely undetected. FIX (this revision, the evaluator's own prescribed technique, mirroring the 046-lineage precedent of entry 4's SPEC BODY + OTHER-FRONTMATTER UNCHANGED HEAD-anchored-exact-comparison approach): entry 10 is rebuilt from a diff-pattern search into a HEAD-anchored exact-transform-and-compare -- read HEAD's tools/validate.sh content verbatim, assert each of the two pinned FROM strings (from scope.files_modify[1].purpose) occurs EXACTLY ONCE in HEAD, apply both FROM->TO substitutions in-memory, and require the result to be byte-identical to the actual working-tree file. This makes ANY deviation from exactly the two authorized substitutions -- heredoc-internal or otherwise, including whitespace-only drift anywhere in the file -- a hard failure, while simultaneously proving both substitutions were applied exactly as contracted (closed-set exact-text pinning, not pattern exclusion). Re-proven, all in a disposable scratch git repository (never touching the real repository tree): (i) all THREE of the evaluator's tampers, reconstructed exactly per the review artifact's own descriptions, now correctly FAIL; (ii) the compliant implementation (exactly the two pinned substitutions, nothing else) correctly PASSES; (iii) a mixed state (both pinned substitutions PLUS the NEGATIVE_TOKEN tamper) correctly FAILS; (iv) whitespace-only drift (a single trailing-space pair introduced on the shebang line, far from either pinned spot) correctly FAILS -- byte-identity is intentionally strict here: for a file shipping inside the v0.7.0 consumer payload to every future adopter, even an incidental editor-introduced whitespace change should be caught and force explicit review rather than being silently tolerated, so no whitespace-normalization exception is carved out. TIMING MODEL (also corrected): unlike revision 0's entry 10, which was a 'stays true' invariant passing identically before and after implementation, this revision's entry 10 is now GATE-DEPENDENT -- pre-implementation the working tree equals HEAD unmodified, so transform(HEAD) (which already differs from HEAD by construction) can never equal the unmodified working tree, and the check correctly FAILS; only once the exact two substitutions land does transform(HEAD) == working-tree, and it correctly PASSES. This is reflected in the corrected expected pre-implementation pattern (entry 10 moves from the revision-0 PASS-now column to FAIL-now).

files_modify.purpose: FIX 1: in the NDEBT-016 row, escape exactly the three raw pipes inside the regex code-span, changing ONLY that span from `(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)` (FROM, verbatim) to `(?:^\|_)(?:neg\|invalid)(?:_\|\.)` (TO, verbatim) -- no other character in the row is touched; the row must continue to read '| NDEBT-016 | 2026-07-17 | Low | ... | ... |' with 'Low' remaining the exact cell-3 content. ADD exactly two new Open rows, NDEBT-021 (Low, ecosystem_preflight.py minimum-CLI hardening backlog) and NDEBT-022 (Medium, ecosystem/07 progress-comparison taxonomy gap), with the planner's texts from planning_notes_047.md Sec 3 verbatim (converted from blockquote to the house table-row format, one physical line per row, no raw unescaped pipes in any cell). Bump frontmatter `version` 0.12.0 -> 0.13.0. DEBT.md carries no `change_log` array in its frontmatter (confirmed absent, schema-optional, matching the precedent established by features 045/046's own DEBT.md edits), so none is added. No existing row (including NDEBT-016's own content beyond the three pipes) is reworded, reordered, or removed.

FIX 2, DOC-ONLY, ZERO behavior change, exactly two spots. Spot A (help text, ~lines 108-109), FROM (verbatim): '--target is the only mode under which files in tools/fixtures/ are ever read.' TO (verbatim, matching the surrounding indentation style): 'Files in tools/fixtures/ are read only under --target and by the default sweep's C12 ecosystem-fixture check.' Spot B (comment, ~line 1336), FROM (verbatim): '(each fixture is already individually inspectable that way)' TO (verbatim): '(each fixture is directly inspectable via python3+jsonschema; --target currently misroutes these families -- NDEBT-015)'. Line 319's phrasing ('...already independently inspectable via a direct `python3`+`jsonschema` invocation...') is the ALREADY-CORRECT, refuted part of the finding and MUST NOT be touched. No function definition, check invocation, or exit-code expression anywhere in the file is touched; the diff is confined to comment/help-text bytes only (structurally proven by entry 10).

non_goals: No edit to any `.agent/evidence/*` file -- immutable point-in-time audit captures.

No edit to `.agent/evidence/045-release-readiness-checklist.md` -- SHA-pinned by contract 045's own verification entry 17; byte-identity must survive this feature untouched (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 23).
No edit to `.agent/run_state.json` -- orchestrator-owned (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 21).
No edit to `docs/planning/manifest.json` (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 22).

No edit to `v0.6.0-release-notes.md` -- pre-existing untracked file, content hash must remain 15ac6acaa9a549a171b5f49617654cac189322fbab760d78104b4c3f4662c00b (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 24).
No edit to `CHANGELOG.md` -- the [0.7.0] heading's release-date staleness (PR-24 item #29) is explicitly a release-day operator note per planning_notes_047.md Sec 5, not this feature's diff.
No edit to `tools/ecosystem_preflight.py` or any `ecosystem/*` protocol document -- NDEBT-021/022 register these as future-phase backlog items, they are not remediated here.
No creation of any git tag, of any kind, under this contract or its implementation step (re-verified live by this contract's own entry 27).
No edit to `.agent/feature_list_005.json` beyond what the planner has already made -- this contract's implementation step does not touch it at all.
No edit to any `.agent/contracts/*.json` other than this `047.json`, or any `.agent/qa/*.json` -- immutable audit records.

No git commit, push, PR creation, PR merge, or branch operation of any kind under this contract or its implementation step -- landing the tip commit is a separate, later pipeline action, per the stacked-PR convention.

No repository change of any kind for the 20 EXPLAIN or 2 DUPLICATE findings -- their resolution is the planner's pre-written PR-reply rationale (.agent/planning_notes_047.md Sec 4), posted by the orchestrator, not a code or document change.

No remediation of any DEBT item's underlying condition -- this contract registers two NEW rows only (NDEBT-021, NDEBT-022) and fixes the NDEBT-016 markdown-parse defect only; it does not touch or resolve any OTHER existing row.

No behavior change of any kind in tools/validate.sh -- both spots are comment/help-text rewordings only; no function, check invocation, or exit expression is added, removed, or altered (structurally proven by entry 10, and by full-sweep re-execution proven behaviorally identical per design_notes.e2e_and_sweep_dry_run_scope).

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored, current tip 6db8363): none of this contract's deliverables already exist -- DEBT.md's NDEBT-016 regex still has raw pipes, version still 0.12.0, NDEBT-021/022 absent; validate.sh's help text and line-1336 comment still carry their original wording.
verification.command:
set -e
git show HEAD:docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -Fq '(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)' || { echo 'raw-pipe NDEBT-016 regex unexpectedly already fixed at HEAD'; exit 1; }
v=$(git show HEAD:docs/planning/DEBT.md | python3 -c "import yaml,sys; print(yaml.safe_load(sys.stdin.read().split('---')[1])['version'])")
[ "$v" = "0.12.0" ] || { echo "expected HEAD DEBT.md version 0.12.0, got $v"; exit 1; }
git show HEAD:docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -Eq 'NDEBT-021|NDEBT-022' && { echo 'NDEBT-021/022 unexpectedly already present at HEAD'; exit 1; }
git show HEAD:tools/validate.sh | python3 -c "
import sys
t = ' '.join(sys.stdin.read().split())
assert '--target is the only mode under which files in tools/fixtures/ are ever read' in t, 'help text unexpectedly already fixed at HEAD'
assert 'each fixture is already individually inspectable that way' in t, 'line-1336 comment unexpectedly already fixed at HEAD'
"
echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-016 STRUCTURAL CELL-COUNT PARSE (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): the row parses to exactly 5 markdown cells with 'NDEBT-016' in cell 1 and 'Low' in cell 3, once the escaped pipes are neutralised before splitting on the real delimiters.
verification.command: python3 -c "import sys; ls=[l for l in open('docs/planning/DEBT.md',encoding='utf-8') if l.lstrip().startswith('| NDEBT-016 ')]; assert ls, 'row missing'; s=ls[0].strip().replace(chr(92)+'|',chr(0)); cells=s.split('|')[1:-1]; sys.exit(0 if len(cells)==5 and cells[0].strip()=='NDEBT-016' and cells[2].strip()=='Low' else 1)"

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-016 ESCAPED-FORM PRESENT + RAW-FORM ABSENT (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim).
verification.command: grep -Fq '(?:^\|_)(?:neg\|invalid)(?:_\|\.)' docs/planning/DEBT.md && ! grep -Fq '(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)' docs/planning/DEBT.md

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-016 ANTI-VACUITY -- MANDATED HEAD-CARET LITERAL (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim; see design_notes.head_caret_timing_note for the pre-commit timing caveat): the raw-pipe form DID exist on the parent commit.
verification.command: git show HEAD^:docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -Fq '(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)'

verification.acceptance_test: VALIDATE.SH HELP TEXT FIXED (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim; whitespace-collapsed per NDEBT-014 class 3, since the phrase spans a wrapped line).
verification.command: python3 -c "import sys; t=' '.join(open('tools/validate.sh',encoding='utf-8').read().split()); bad='--target is the only mode under which files in tools/fixtures/ are ever read'; ok='Files in tools/fixtures/ are read only under --target and by the default sweep'; sys.exit(0 if bad not in t and ok in t else 1)"

verification.acceptance_test: VALIDATE.SH HELP TEXT ANTI-VACUITY -- MANDATED HEAD-CARET LITERAL (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): the inaccurate help sentence DID exist on the parent commit.
verification.command: git show HEAD^:tools/validate.sh | python3 -c "import sys; t=' '.join(sys.stdin.read().split()); sys.exit(0 if '--target is the only mode under which files in tools/fixtures/ are ever read' in t else 1)"

verification.acceptance_test: VALIDATE.SH LINE-1336 COMMENT FIXED (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim; whitespace-collapsed): the old wording is gone and NDEBT-015 is now referenced.
verification.command: python3 -c "import sys; t=' '.join(open('tools/validate.sh',encoding='utf-8').read().split()); old='each fixture is already individually inspectable that way'; sys.exit(0 if old not in t and 'NDEBT-015' in t else 1)"

verification.acceptance_test: VALIDATE.SH LINE-1336 ANTI-VACUITY -- HEAD-CARET COMPANION (this contract's own addition, mirroring the help-text companion's symmetry): the old line-1336 wording DID exist on the parent commit.
verification.command: git show HEAD^:tools/validate.sh | python3 -c "import sys; t=' '.join(sys.stdin.read().split()); sys.exit(0 if 'each fixture is already individually inspectable that way' in t else 1)"

verification.acceptance_test: LINE-319 ALREADY-CORRECT PHRASING PRESERVED UNTOUCHED (CORRECTED from feature_list_005.json's own acceptance test 10 -- see design_notes.line319_literal_discrepancy for the discovered discrepancy and why the literal is corrected rather than reproduced verbatim): the refuted-finding phrase, whitespace-collapsed, is unchanged.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
t = ' '.join(open('tools/validate.sh', encoding='utf-8').read().split())
pinned = 'already independently inspectable via a direct \`python3\`+\`jsonschema\` invocation'
assert pinned in t, 'line-319 already-correct phrasing changed or missing -- forbidden to touch'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: VALIDATE.SH DIFF IS EXACT-TRANSFORM BYTE-IDENTICAL (REVISED at revision 1 per @evaluator round-0 HIGH finding -- see design_notes.exact_transform_fix; this REPLACES the revision-0 diff-pattern-exclusion guard, which was structurally blind to heredoc-internal behavioral changes): HEAD's tools/validate.sh, with EXACTLY the two pinned FROM->TO substitutions applied in-memory (each asserted to occur exactly once in HEAD), must be byte-identical to the working-tree file. This is a GATE-DEPENDENT check (revision-0's entry 10 was a 'stays true' invariant; this revision's entry 10 is now 'becomes true' -- pre-implementation the working tree equals HEAD unmodified, so the transformed-HEAD comparison correctly FAILS; only after the exact two substitutions land does it PASS). Any change anywhere in the file outside the two authorized spots -- including inside a check function's embedded heredoc body -- breaks byte-equality and is therefore a hard failure, closing the exact gap the evaluator's three tampers (narrowed NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex, narrowed fixture glob, defanged NDEBT-009 dormancy guard) demonstrated against the revision-0 guard.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import subprocess
head_text = subprocess.check_output(['git', 'show', 'HEAD:tools/validate.sh']).decode()
work_text = open('tools/validate.sh', encoding='utf-8').read()

from_a = '                      applicable check fails. --target is the only mode\n                      under which files in tools/fixtures/ are ever read.\n'
to_a   = '                      applicable check fails. Files in tools/fixtures/ are\n                      read only under --target and by the default sweep\'s\n                      C12 ecosystem-fixture check.\n'
from_b = '# under --target (each fixture is already individually inspectable that way)\n'
to_b   = '# under --target (each fixture is directly inspectable via\n# python3+jsonschema; --target currently misroutes these families --\n# NDEBT-015)\n'

assert head_text.count(from_a) == 1, f'Spot A FROM text must occur exactly once in HEAD, found {head_text.count(from_a)}'
assert head_text.count(from_b) == 1, f'Spot B FROM text must occur exactly once in HEAD, found {head_text.count(from_b)}'

transformed = head_text.replace(from_a, to_a, 1).replace(from_b, to_b, 1)
assert transformed == work_text, 'working-tree tools/validate.sh is not byte-identical to HEAD-with-exactly-the-two-pinned-substitutions-applied -- unauthorized change detected'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md NDEBT-021/022 ROW REGISTRATION, EXACT SEVERITIES (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): NDEBT-021 is exactly Low, NDEBT-022 is exactly Medium.
verification.command: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('docs/planning/DEBT.md',encoding='utf-8').read(); rows={m.group(1):m.group(2).strip() for m in re.finditer(r'\|\s*(NDEBT-0(?:21|22))\s*\|[^|]*\|\s*(Low|Medium|High|P0|P1)\s*\|', t)}; sys.exit(0 if set(rows)=={'NDEBT-021','NDEBT-022'} and rows['NDEBT-021']=='Low' and rows['NDEBT-022']=='Medium' else 1)"

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md VERSION BUMP (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim): frontmatter version changed from 0.12.0.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=yaml.safe_load(open('docs/planning/DEBT.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]); sys.exit(0 if str(d['version'])!='0.12.0' else 1)"

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md ROW-COUNT-DELTA BOUND (this contract's own addition, proactively applying the contract-046-evaluator lesson: a closed-set ID check alone cannot detect an unauthorized THIRD row; HEAD-anchored): total '| NDEBT-' row count in the working tree is exactly 2 more than at HEAD.
verification.command:
head_count=$(git show HEAD:docs/planning/DEBT.md | grep -c '^| NDEBT-')
work_count=$(grep -c '^| NDEBT-' docs/planning/DEBT.md)
delta=$((work_count - head_count))
[ "$delta" -eq 2 ] || { echo "expected exactly 2 new NDEBT rows (HEAD had $head_count, working tree has $work_count, delta=$delta)"; exit 1; }
echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md ROW CONTENT SUBSTANTIVE CHECK (this contract's own addition; whole-row-line matching, not column-position regex, per the contract-046 embedded-pipe-defect lesson): NDEBT-021's row names ecosystem_preflight.py and fail-closed substance; NDEBT-022's row names ecosystem/07 and taxonomy substance.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re
t = open('docs/planning/DEBT.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
lines = t.splitlines()
line21 = next((l for l in lines if re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-021 *\|', l)), None)
assert line21, 'NDEBT-021 row not found'
assert ('ecosystem_preflight.py' in line21) and ('fail' in line21.lower()), 'NDEBT-021 row missing ecosystem_preflight.py / fail-closed substance'
line22 = next((l for l in lines if re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-022 *\|', l)), None)
assert line22, 'NDEBT-022 row not found'
assert ('ecosystem/07' in line22) and (('taxonomy' in line22.lower()) or ('non-exhaustive' in line22.lower())), 'NDEBT-022 row missing ecosystem/07 taxonomy substance'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT.md NEW ROWS CONTAIN NO RAW UNESCAPED PIPES (this contract's own addition, per the planner's explicit instruction not to reintroduce the exact defect FIX 1 corrects): both new rows parse to exactly 5 cells after escape-neutralisation.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re
t = open('docs/planning/DEBT.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
lines = t.splitlines()
for rid in ('NDEBT-021', 'NDEBT-022'):
    line = next((l for l in lines if re.match(rf'^\| *{rid} *\|', l)), None)
    assert line, f'{rid} row not found'
    stripped = line.replace('\\\\|', '')
    cells = stripped.split('|')[1:-1]
    assert len(cells) == 5, f'{rid} row does not parse to 5 cells after escape-neutralisation (got {len(cells)})'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: .agent IMMUTABILITY GUARD (house two-part invariant: tracked-history-clean + untracked-additions-confined-to-glob; whitelist explicitly includes the planner's own pre-existing .agent/feature_list_005.json edit, applying the contract-046 Mode-A blocker lesson proactively -- this feature's own deliverables are NOT under .agent/, so no contract-owned tracked path is added to the tolerance): no existing tracked .agent file is modified other than feature_list_005.json; no new untracked .agent path exists outside this feature's own contract/qa/evidence artifacts and the planner's own planning_notes_047.md.
verification.command:
tracked_bad=0
while IFS= read -r path; do
  [ -z "$path" ] && continue
  case "$path" in
    .agent/feature_list_005.json) ;;
    *) echo "out-of-scope tracked .agent modification: $path"; tracked_bad=1 ;;
  esac
done <<< "$(git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent)"
[ "$tracked_bad" -eq 0 ] || exit 1

untracked_bad=0
while IFS= read -r line; do
  [ -z "$line" ] && continue
  case "$line" in
    '?? '*) ;;
    *) continue ;;
  esac
  path="${line:3}"
  case "$path" in
    .agent/contracts/047.json|.agent/qa/047*.json|.agent/evidence/047-*|.agent/evidence/047/*|.agent/planning_notes_047.md) ;;
    *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent artifact: $path"; untracked_bad=1 ;;
  esac
done <<< "$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent)"
[ "$untracked_bad" -eq 0 ] && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: SCOPE GUARD (this contract's own vlib_scope_guard-based check, .agent-excluding by design and dry-run proven clean against the current tree; paired with a non-blocking note on the mandated HEAD-caret..HEAD tip-diff literal's post-commit timing -- see design_notes.head_caret_timing_note): only the 2 contracted files (plus the pre-existing tolerated v0.6.0-release-notes.md) are changed outside .agent/.
verification.command:
source tools/verify_lib.sh
vlib_scope_guard "docs/planning/DEBT.md" "tools/validate.sh" "v0.6.0-release-notes.md" || { echo 'vlib_scope_guard reported out-of-scope changed path(s)'; exit 1; }
echo "NOTE (non-blocking, pre-commit): the mandated HEAD-caret..HEAD tip-diff-confinement literal is timing-deferred until this feature is committed as the single stack-tip commit -- see design_notes.head_caret_timing_note"
echo PASS

verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0 && echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "default sweep failed (rc=$rc)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: PAYLOAD COMPLIANCE SWEEP GREEN, EXIT STATUS ASSERTED (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim; the '(payload mode)' qualifier is already correct this time, per the feature-046 correction it cites).
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0 && echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY \(payload mode\): 10 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "payload sweep failed (rc=$rc)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: E2E BOOTSTRAP TEST UNAFFECTED (feature_list_005.json's own mandated literal, run verbatim; see design_notes.e2e_and_sweep_dry_run_scope for why full post-implementation execution is necessarily Mode-B/QA's responsibility, not this contract's own pre-implementation dry run, since the test requires real git history this contract must not create).

fi

verification.acceptance_test: CROSS-FEATURE INVARIANT -- 045's ZERO-OPEN-P0/P1 DEBT SCAN STILL GREEN (re-run against the DEBT.md table INCLUDING this feature's two new rows): zero Open High/Critical rows anywhere in the Open section.

CROSS-FEATURE INVARIANT -- 046's NDEBT-019/020 ROWS PRESERVED UNMODIFIED (this contract's own addition, per the coordinator's explicit request to verify the new rows don't disturb 046's prior registrations): NDEBT-019 stays Low with its pipefail substance intact; NDEBT-020 stays Medium with its rm -f substance intact.
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re
t = open('docs/planning/DEBT.md', encoding='utf-8').read()
lines = t.splitlines()
line19 = next((l for l in lines if re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-019 *\|', l)), None)
line20 = next((l for l in lines if re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-020 *\|', l)), None)
assert line19, 'NDEBT-019 row missing'
assert line20, 'NDEBT-020 row missing'
m19 = re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-019 *\| *[^|]*\| *([^|]*)\|', line19)
m20 = re.match(r'^\| *NDEBT-020 *\| *[^|]*\| *([^|]*)\|', line20)
assert m19 and m19.group(1).strip() == 'Low', 'NDEBT-019 severity changed'
assert m20 and m20.group(1).strip() == 'Medium', 'NDEBT-020 severity changed'
assert 'pipefail' in line19, 'NDEBT-019 substance text changed'
assert 'rm -f' in line20, 'NDEBT-020 substance text changed'
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: NO-SELF-TAG INVARIANT: tag count stays 10 and v0.7.0 remains absent.
verification.command:
before_count=10
after=$(git tag -l | wc -l)
[ "$after" -eq "$before_count" ] || { echo "tag count changed: expected $before_count, got $after"; exit 1; }
git tag -l 'v0.7.0' | grep -q . && { echo 'v0.7.0 tag was created -- forbidden'; exit 1; } || echo PASS

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/047-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (the 'verification' array has exactly 27 entries, so .agent/evidence/047-verify-01.txt through .agent/evidence/047-verify-27.txt). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED at revision 0 by the evaluator. One HIGH-severity finding: entry 10 (the VALIDATE.SH DIFF IS DOC-ONLY guard), together with the sweep-green invariant, was this contract's entire mechanical proof of its central 'zero behavior change' claim for tools/validate.sh -- a file shipping inside the v0.7.0 consumer payload -- but the revision-0 diff-pattern-exclusion guard was structurally blind to changes made inside a check function's own embedded heredoc body. Three real tampers (narrowed NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex; narrowed fixture glob; defanged NDEBT-009 dormancy guard) all falsely passed revision-0's entry 10, and the third was additionally invisible to the entire 27-entry suite (no shipped fixture triggers the dormancy guard's condition either way today). FIX (this revision, the evaluator's own prescribed technique): entry 10 rebuilt as a HEAD-anchored exact-transform-and-compare -- apply the two already-pinned FROM->TO substitutions to HEAD's content in-memory (each asserted to occur exactly once) and require byte-identity with the working tree, closing every heredoc-internal and whitespace-drift vector at once. Re-proven against all three of the evaluator's tampers (all now FAIL), the compliant implementation (PASSES), a mixed compliant+tamper state (FAILS), and whitespace-only drift (FAILS, confirmed intentional for a payload-shipped file). The expected pre-implementation pattern is corrected: entry 10 moves from a 'stays true' invariant to a 'becomes true' gate-dependent check (now correctly FAILS pre-implementation). design_notes.head_caret_timing_note updated to reflect entry 10 no longer being HEAD-caret-timing-deferred. status remains 'proposed'; scope, non_goals, and estimated_lines (13) are unchanged; entries 1-9 and 11-27 are byte-identical to revision 0.
